name: Case Study - Janice Jordan - LRPS | RPS Distinctions

step.name: Why did you want to gain a distinction with the Royal Photographic Society (RPS)?
step.text: It seemed the next natural step. I’m best motivated by having a project so this was ideal for me. It was also a way for me to understand and focus on areas in which I needed to improve.

step.name: Describe how you found the RPS Distinctions Mentoring Classes.
step.text: I can’t really fault the format of the mentoring classes as they are restricted to a small group of participants which means that Alan is able to give each participant the right amount of time. Being able to listen to Alan’s critique and advice to the other participants is also really useful.

step.name: What distinction did you apply for and were you successful?
step.text: I applied for the L and was successful.

step.name: What was your experience like of the RPS Assessment Day?
step.text: I attended the assessment day on Zoom as I couldn’t make the trip to Bristol. Luckily I was first on, so I didn’t have to wait long in my “calmly stressed” state. I was pleasantly surprised at the positive feedback I had from the assessors, and it has certainly given me more confidence in my ability.

step.name: Did you seek independent advice, feedback or input from anywhere else before your panel assessment?
step.text: I booked a 1:1 with an RPS assessor to review my draft panel. This was invaluable as it gave an insight into what the assessors would be looking for. The advice given certainly helped me refine the panel layout and images to bring it up to the required standard

step.name: Having completed the course and Distinction Assessment, what advice would you like to share with others?
step.text: Use the checklist provided by Alan, which helps you review the images you have and highlights the gaps you need to fill. Also, having taken part in an assessment day, my advice would be to have your panel critiqued by an RPS assessor before submission to give you the best chance of being successful.

step.name: What was the hardest part of gaining a distinction with the RPS and why?
step.text: Staying motivated and also processing images to the required standard. Sometimes having to go back and start processing again.
